method_atom: A system of N coupled integrable nonlinear wave equations with mixed-sign self-interaction (some components self-focusing, others self-defocusing), solved exactly by a bilinearization technique that yields two complementary families of localized traveling-wave excitations — 'elevation' type on a zero background and 'depression' type as a notch on a nonzero background — which for N>2 can be partitioned among the components in multiple ways. Exact analysis of pairwise collisions shows that when enough elevation-type modes populate two or more components the collision is non-elastic, redistributing amplitude/intensity across components with an amplitude-dependent phase shift and a change in relative separation, while the depression-type modes collide elastically yet inherit the identical amplitude-dependent phase shift, so the two families mutually condition each other's scattering.
object_atom: Mixed bright-dark soliton solutions of the integrable N-coupled nonlinear Schrödinger equations with mixed focusing/defocusing nonlinearity coefficients, i.e. multi-component envelope solitons in nonlinear media such as multimode optical fibers or multi-component condensates.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the exact-integrable coupled-equation machinery and its elastic-vs-shape-changing collision structure with amplitude-dependent phase shifts; the domain-specific part is the bright/dark envelope solitons of the coupled nonlinear Schrödinger fields.
